title: Collabora Office 26.04 adds optional, user-controlled AI to desktop documents
friendlyTitle: Collabora adds AI that users can control
summary: Collabora Office 26.04 brings the suite’s online codebase further onto Windows, macOS, and Linux, with an AI sidebar for Writer, Calc, and Impress. The AI is disabled by default, lets users choose their own provider or self-hosted model, and requires approval before reading from or writing to documents.
friendlySummary: Collabora Office now has optional AI help for documents, spreadsheets, and presentations. Users choose the AI provider, and the feature starts turned off.
whyItMatters: Office AI is moving from cloud-only copilots toward user-controlled integrations. That gives privacy-sensitive teams a path to document assistance without handing all content to a default vendor model.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This is useful for people and companies that want AI help with documents but still want control over where their data goes.
